title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Independent Query Trial
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a versioned read model and audited transition design with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab View Contracts
steps.instruction:
lab case — Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 104. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab View Contracts.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test trigger side effects, recursion guards, changed rows, rollback, and accessible audit evidence.
